components.name: A01NYUB Waterproof Ultrasonic Sensor
components.description: This sensor allows you to use A01NYUB waterproof ultrasonic sensor by DFRobot ([datasheet](https://wiki.dfrobot.com/A01NYUB%20Waterproof%20Ultrasonic%20Sensor%20SKU_SEN0313)) with ESPHome to measure distances. This sensor can measure ranges between 28 centimeters and 750 centimeters with a resolution of 1 millimeter.

components.name: A02YYUW Waterproof Ultrasonic Sensor
components.description: This sensor allows you to use A02YYUW waterproof ultrasonic sensor by DFRobot ([datasheet](https://wiki.dfrobot.com/_A02YYUW_Waterproof_Ultrasonic_Sensor_SKU_SEN0311)) with ESPHome to measure distances. This sensor can measure ranges between 3 centimeters and 450 centimeters with a resolution of 1 millimeter.

components.name: Stepper Component
components.description: Put this code into the configuration file on ESPHome for this device.

components.name: Absolute Humidity
components.description: The `absolute_humidity` platform allows you to calculate absolute humidity from air temperature and relative humidity.

components.name: AC Dimmer Component
components.description: The `ac_dimmer` component allows you to connect a dimmable light or other load which supports phase control dimming to your ESPHome project.

components.name: Analog To Digital Sensor
components.description: The Analog To Digital (`adc`  ) Sensor allows you to use the built-in ADC in your device to measure a voltage on certain pins.

components.name: ADC128S102 8-Channel 12-Bit A/D Converter
components.description: The ADC128S102 is a low-power, eight-channel CMOS 12-bit analog-to-digital converter specified for conversion throughput rates of 500 ksps to 1 MSPS. The converter is based on a successive-approximation register architecture with an internal track-and-hold circuit.

components.name: ADC128S102 8-Channel 12-Bit A/D Converter
components.description: The `adc128s102` sensor allows you to use your ADC128S102 12-Bit A/D Converter sensors with ESPHome. First, setup a [ADC128S102 Hub](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/adc128s102#adc128s102-component) for your ADC128S102 sensor and then use this sensor platform to create individual sensors that will report the voltage to Home Assistant.

components.name: Addressable Light
components.description: The `addressable_light` display platform allows to display text and graphics on an addressable light that has been arranged in a display matrix.

components.name: ADE7880 Power Sensor
components.description: The `ade7880` sensor platform allows you to use ADE7880 voltage/current/power sensors ([datasheet](https://www.analog.com/media/en/technical-documentation/data-sheets/ADE7880.pdf)) with ESPHome. This sensor chip is commonly found in Shelly 3EM and 3EM Pro devices.

components.name: ADE7953 Power Sensor
components.description: None

components.name: ADS1115 4-Channel 16-Bit A/D Converter
components.description: The `ads1115` domain creates a global hub so that you can later create individual sensors using the [ADS1115 Sensor Platform](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/ads1115#ads1115-sensor). To use this hub, first setup the [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) and connect the sensor to the pins specified there.

components.name: ADS1115 4-Channel 16-Bit A/D Converter
components.description: The `ads1115` sensor allows you to use your ADS1115 sigma-delta ADC sensors ([datasheet](http://www.ti.com/lit/ds/symlink/ads1115.pdf), `Adafruit_ADS1115`*) or your ADS1015 sigma-delta ADC sensors ([datasheet](http://www.ti.com/lit/ds/symlink/ads1015.pdf), `Adafruit_ADS1015`*) with ESPHome. First, setup an [ADS1115 Hub](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/ads1115#ads1115-component) for your ADS1115 sensor and then use this sensor platform to create individual sensors that will report the voltage to Home Assistant.

components.name: ADS1118 4-Channel 16-Bit A/D Converter with Internal Temperature Sensor
components.description: ADS1118 4-Channel 16-Bit A/D Converter ([datasheet](https://www.ti.com/lit/ds/symlink/ads1118.pdf)) The `ads1118` domain creates a global hub so that you can later create individual sensors using the [ADS1118 Sensor Platform](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/ads1118#ads1118-sensor). It uses the [SPI Bus](https://esphome.io/components/spi) for communication.

components.name: ADS1118 4-Channel 16-Bit A/D Converter with Internal Temperature Sensor
components.description: The `ads1118` sensor allows you to use your ADS1118 delta-sigma ADC sensors ([datasheet](https://www.ti.com/lit/ds/symlink/ads1118.pdf)) with ESPHome. First, setup an [ADS1118 Hub](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/ads1118#ads1118-component) for your ADS1118 sensor and then use this sensor platform to create individual sensors that will report the voltage.

components.name: AGS10 Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) Sensor
components.description: The `ags10` sensor platform VOC sensor allows you to use your ASAIR AGS10 ([datasheet](http://www.aosong.com/userfiles/files/Datasheet%20AGS10.pdf), [ASAIR](http://www.aosong.com/en/products-86.html)) sensors with ESPHome. The [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: AHT10 Temperature+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The `aht10` Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your AHT10 ([datasheet](http://www.aosong.com/userfiles/files/media/aht10%E8%A7%84%E6%A0%BC%E4%B9%A6v1_1%EF%BC%8820191015%EF%BC%89.pdf)), AHT20 ([datasheet](https://cdn-learn.adafruit.com/assets/assets/000/091/676/original/AHT20-datasheet-2020-4-16.pdf?1591047915)) or AHT30 ([datasheet](https://eleparts.co.kr/data/goods_attach/202306/good-pdf-12751003-1.pdf)) [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c)-based sensor with ESPHome.

components.name: AIC3204
components.description: The `aic3204` platform allows your ESPHome devices to use the AIC3204 ultra low power stereo audio codec (DAC). This allows the playback of audio via the microcontroller from a range of sources via [Media Player](https://esphome.io/components/media_player/).

components.name: AirThings BLE Sensors
components.description: The `airthings_wave_plus` and `airthings_wave_mini` sensor platforms lets you track the output of AirThings Bluetooth Low Energy devices.

components.name: Grundfos Alpha3
components.description: The `alpha3` component allows you to monitor a Grundfos Alpha3 Model B circulator pump over BLE.

components.name: AM2315C Temperature+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The `am2315c` Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your AM2315C ([datasheet](https://cdn-shop.adafruit.com/product-files/5182/5182_AM2315C.pdf)) I²C-based sensor with ESPHome.

components.name: AM2320 Temperature+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The `am2320` Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your AM2320 ([datasheet](https://cdn-shop.adafruit.com/product-files/3721/AM2320.pdf)) I²C-based sensor with ESPHome.

components.name: AM43 Cover
components.description: The `am43` cover platform allows you to control an AM43 based BLE cover motor. These devices are sold under various brands, such as Zemismart and A-OK. The platform connects to the device over the ESP32's BLE peripheral and both controls and fetches the position and state of the motor.

components.name: AM43 Sensor
components.description: The `am43` sensor platform allows you to read the light and battery sensors on an AM43 BLE cover motor. The platform connects to the device over the ESP32's BLE peripheral.

components.name: Analog Threshold Binary Sensor
components.description: The `analog_threshold` binary sensor platform allows you to convert analog values (i.e. [sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/) readings) into boolean values, using a threshold as a reference. When the signal is above or equal to the threshold the binary sensor is `true` (this behavior can be changed by adding an `invert` filter).

components.name: Animation
components.description: Allows to use animated images on displays. Animation inherits all options from the image component. It adds additional lambda methods: `next_frame()`, `prev_frame()` and `set_frame()` to change the shown picture of a gif.

components.name: Anova Cooker
components.description: The `anova` climate platform creates a climate device which can be used to control a BLE Anova sous-vide stick. Whilst not strictly a climate device, the concept of keeping something to a set temperature applies equally here.

components.name: APDS9306 Sensor
components.description: The `apds9306` sensor component allows you to use APDS9306 ambient light sensors ([datasheet](https://docs.broadcom.com/doc/AV02-4755EN), [Broadcom](https://www.broadcom.com/products/optical-sensors/ambient-light-photo-sensors/apds-9306-065)) with ESPHome. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: APDS9960 Sensor
components.description: The `apds9960` sensor platform allows you to use your APDS9960 RGB and gesture sensors ([datasheet](https://cdn.sparkfun.com/datasheets/Sensors/Proximity/apds9960.pdf), [SparkFun](https://www.sparkfun.com/products/12787)) with ESPHome. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: APDS9960 Sensor
components.description: The `apds9960` binary sensor allows you to use your [Apds9960](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/apds9960/) to perform different measurements.

components.name: APDS9960 Sensor
components.description: The `apds9960` sensor allows you to use your [Apds9960](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/apds9960/) to perform different measurements.

components.name: Native API Component
components.description: The ESPHome native API is used to communicate with clients directly, with a highly-optimized network protocol. Currently, only the ESPHome tool, Home Assistant and ioBroker use this native API.

components.name: Air Quality Index (AQI)
components.description: The `aqi` sensor platform allows you to compute an Air Quality Index from PM2.5 and PM10 particulate matter sensor readings. This sensor works with any PM sensor source, such as [Pmsx003](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/pmsx003/), [Hm3301](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/hm3301/), [Sds011](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/sds011/), or [Sps30](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/sps30/).

components.name: AMS AS3935 Franklin Lightning Sensor
components.description: Binary sensor that indicates if a lightning strike was detected.

components.name: AMS AS3935 Franklin Lightning Sensor
components.description: A sensor platform to read lightning data

components.name: AMS AS3935 Franklin Lightning Sensor
components.description: The `as3935_spi` sensor platform allows you to use your AS3935 sensor ([AMS AS3935](https://ams.com/as3935)) in order to get notified when a thunderstorm is getting close.

components.name: AS5600 12-Bit Magnetic Position Sensor
components.description: The `as5600` sensor platform you to use your AS5600 ([datasheet](https://ams.com/documents/20143/36005/AS5600_DS000365_5-00.pdf/649ee61c-8f9a-20df-9e10-43173a3eb323), [AMS](https://ams.com/en/as5600)) or AS5600L ([datasheet](https://ams.com/documents/20143/36005/AS5600L_DS000545_3-00.pdf/7ade6878-7a32-2294-b88d-479d50fab6de), [AMS](https://ams.com/en/as5600l)) 12-bit magnetic position sensor with ESPHome. Individual sensors will be added using the [AS5600 Sensor Platform](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/as5600#as5600-sensor). To use this hub, first setup the [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) and connect the sensor to the pins specified there.

components.name: AS5600 12-Bit Magnetic Position Sensor
components.description: The `as5600` sensor allows you to publish the angle/position measurement from your AS5600 with ESPHome. First, setup an [AS5600 Hub](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/as5600#as5600-component) for your AS5600 sensor and then use this sensor platform to create individual sensors that will report the position to Home Assistant.

components.name: AS7341 Spectral Color Sensor
components.description: The `as7341` sensor platform allows you to use your AS7341 spectral color sensor ([datasheet](https://look.ams-osram.com/m/24266a3e584de4db/original/AS7341-DS000504.pdf), [Adafruit](https://www.adafruit.com/product/4698)) with ESPHome. The [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: AirTouch AT581x Radar
components.description: The [AirTouch AT581x radar](https://en.airtouching.com/product/32.html) (aka `AT581x`  ) is a family of 5.8GHz radar which can be used for human presence detection. It can detect tiny movements and compared to a PIR sensor **it can detect presence continuously**. This can be useful, for example, to turn the lights on when you enter a room, keep them on as long as you are there (without waving your hands at the sensor) and turn them off almost immediately after you leave the room.

components.name: AirTouch AT581x Radar
components.description: [Switch components](https://esphome.io/components/switch#config-switch) are used to enable/disable radio frequency hardware.

components.name: ATM90E26 Power Sensor
components.description: The `atm90e26` sensor platform allows you to use your ATM90E26 voltage/current and power sensors ([datasheet](https://ww1.microchip.com/downloads/en/DeviceDoc/Atmel-46002-SE-M90E26-Datasheet.pdf)) with ESPHome. This sensor is found in the [DitroniX GTEM ESP32](https://ditronix.net/wiki/gtem-esp32-atm90e26-sdk-v1-specification/) energy meter and other devices.

components.name: ATM90E32 Power Sensor
components.description: None

components.name: ATM90E32 Power Sensor
components.description: `number` entities can be configured to provide calibration voltage & current reference values at runtime.

components.name: ATM90E32 Power Sensor
components.description: The `atm90e32` sensor platform allows you to use your ATM90E32 voltage/current and power sensors ([datasheet](http://ww1.microchip.com/downloads/en/devicedoc/Atmel-46003-SE-M90E32AS-Datasheet.pdf)) with ESPHome. This sensor is commonly found in CircuitSetup 2 and 6 channel energy meters and the [Gelidus Research](https://www.gelidus.ca/) 2 channel power meter.

components.name: ATM90E32 Power Sensor
components.description: None

components.name: Audio Codec Configuration
components.description: This component only works on ESP32 based chips.

components.name: Audio File Media Source
components.description: The `audio_file` media source platform plays audio files that have been embedded into the firmware using the [Audio File](https://esphome.io/components/audio_file/) component.

components.name: Audio File Media Source
components.description: Audio File media source in ESPHome.

components.name: Audio HTTP Media Source
components.description: The `audio_http` media source platform plays audio streamed from HTTP and HTTPS URLs. Audio is fetched over the network and decoded on the device.

components.name: AXS15231 Touch Screen Controller
components.description: The `axs15231` touchscreen platform allows using the touch screen controllers based on the AXS15231 chip with ESPHome. An [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this touchscreen to work.

components.name: b-parasite
components.description: [b-parasite](https://github.com/rbaron/b-parasite) is an open source soil moisture and ambient temperature/humidity/light sensor.

components.name: Bang Bang Climate Controller
components.description: The `bang_bang` climate platform allows you to regulate a value with a [bang-bang controller](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bang%E2%80%93bang_control) (also called hysteresis controller).

components.name: BedJet
components.description: The `bedjet` component allows you to communicate with a BedJet V3 Climate Comfort Sleep System.

components.name: BedJet
components.description: The `climate` platform exposes the BedJet's climate-related functionality, including setting the mode and target temperature.

components.name: BedJet
components.description: The `fan` platform exposes the BedJet's fan-related functionality, including on/off and speed control.

components.name: BedJet
components.description: The `sensor` platform exposes the BedJet's various temperature readings as sensors.

components.name: Beken SPI LED Strip
components.description: This is a component using the Beken SPI DMA interface to drive addressable LED strips.

components.name: BH1750 Ambient Light Sensor
components.description: The `bh1750` sensor platform allows you to use your BH1750 ([datasheet](http://www.mouser.com/ds/2/348/bh1750fvi-e-186247.pdf)) ambient light sensor with ESPHome. The [I²C bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BH1900NUX Temperature Sensor
components.description: The `bh1900nux` sensor platform allows you to use the **BH1900NUX** ([datasheet](https://fscdn.rohm.com/en/products/databook/datasheet/ic/sensor/temperature/bh1900nux-e.pdf)) **temperature sensor** from Rohm Semiconductor with ESPHome. The [I²C bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) must be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Binary Fan
components.description: The `binary` fan platform lets you represent any binary [Output Component](https://esphome.io/components/output/) as a fan.

components.name: Binary Light
components.description: The `binary` light platform creates a simple ON/OFF-only light from a [binary output component](https://esphome.io/components/output/).

components.name: Binary Sensor Map
components.description: The `binary_sensor_map` sensor platform allows you to map multiple [binary sensor](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor/) to an individual value. Depending on the state of each binary sensor, its associated configured parameters, and this sensor's mapping type, the `binary_sensor_map` publishes a single numerical value.

components.name: Belling BL0906 Energy Monitor
components.description: The `bl0906` sensor platform allows you to use your BL0906 voltage/current/power and energy sensors with ESPHome. This sensor is commonly found in [Athom EM2 Energy Meter](https://www.athom.tech/blank-1/2-ch-energy-meter-made-for-esphome) and [Athom EM6 Energy Meter](https://www.athom.tech/blank-1/6-ch-energy-meter-made-for-esphome)

components.name: BL0939 Power Sensor
components.description: The `bl0939` sensor platform allows you to use your BL0939 voltage/current/power and energy sensors with ESPHome. This sensor is commonly found in Sonoff Dual R3 v2.

components.name: Belling BL0940 Energy Monitor
components.description: The `bl0940` button platform allows to reset all external calibration values to their initial state.

components.name: Belling BL0940 Energy Monitor
components.description: The `bl0940` number platform allows for online calibration of the sensor readings.

components.name: Belling BL0940 Energy Monitor
components.description: None

components.name: Belling BL0942 Energy Monitor
components.description: The `bl0942` sensor platform allows you to use BL0942 energy monitors sensors with ESPHome. These are used in some Tuya-devices (e.g. Aubess Tuya WiFi Mini Smart Switch With Energy Monitor)

components.name: BLE Client
components.description: The `ble_client` component enables connections to Bluetooth Low Energy devices in order to query and control them. This component does not expose any sensors or output components itself, but merely manages connections to them for use by other components.

components.name: BLE Client Binary Output
components.description: Writes a binary value to a BLE device.

components.name: BLE Client Sensor
components.description: Fetch numeric values from BLE devices.

components.name: BLE Client Switch
components.description: The `ble_client` component enables connections to Bluetooth Low Energy devices in order to query and control them. This component does not expose any sensors or output components itself, but merely manages connections to them for use by other components.

components.name: BLE Client Text Sensor
components.description: Fetch string values from BLE devices.

components.name: Nordic UART Service (NUS)
components.description: The BLE NUS component provides a Bluetooth Low Energy UART interface based on the Nordic UART Service. It can be used to stream logs or enable custom bidirectional communication with ESPHome.

components.name: ESP32 Bluetooth Low Energy Device
components.description: The `ble_presence` binary sensor platform lets you track the presence of a Bluetooth Low Energy device.

components.name: ESP32 Bluetooth Low Energy RSSI Sensor
components.description: The `ble_rssi` sensor platform lets you track the RSSI value or signal strength of a BLE device. See [the binary sensor setup](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor/ble_presence#esp32_ble_tracker-setting_up_devices) for instructions for setting up this platform.

components.name: ESP32 Bluetooth Low Energy Scanner
components.description: The `ble_scanner` text sensor platform lets you track reachable BLE devices.

components.name: Bluetooth Proxy
components.description: Home Assistant can expand its Bluetooth reach by communicating through the Bluetooth proxy component in ESPHome. The individual device integrations in Home Assistant (such as BTHome) will receive the data from the Bluetooth Integration in Home Assistant which automatically aggregates all ESPHome Bluetooth proxies with any USB Bluetooth Adapters you might have. This exceptional feature offers fault tolerant connection between the Bluetooth devices and Home Assistant.

components.name: BM8563 Time Source
components.description: You first need to set up the [I2C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c/) component.

components.name: BME680 Temperature+Pressure+Humidity+Gas Sensor
components.description: The `bme680` sensor platform allows you to use your BME680 ([datasheet](https://cdn-shop.adafruit.com/product-files/3660/BME680.pdf), [Adafruit](https://www.adafruit.com/product/3660)) temperature, pressure and humidity sensors with ESPHome. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BME680 Temperature+Pressure+Humidity+Gas Sensor via BSEC
components.description: The `bme680_bsec` sensor platform allows you to use your BME680 ([datasheet](https://www.bosch-sensortec.com/media/boschsensortec/downloads/datasheets/bst-bme680-ds001.pdf), [Adafruit](https://www.adafruit.com/product/3660), [Pimoroni](https://shop.pimoroni.com/products/bme680-breakout)) temperature, pressure and humidity and gas sensors with ESPHome via the Bosch Sensortec Environmental Cluster (BSEC) software library. The use of Bosch's proprietary algorithms provide an Index for Air Quality (IAQ) measurement derived from the gas resistance sensor's response to specific Volatile Organic Compounds (VOC). The BSEC software also provides estimated values for CO₂ and Breath Volatile Organic Compounds (b-VOC) using a correlation between VOC and CO₂ in a human's exhaled breath.

components.name: BME680 Temperature+Pressure+Humidity+Gas Sensor via BSEC

components.name: BP1658CJ LED driver
components.description: The BP1658CJ output component exposes a BP1658CJ channel of a global [Component/Hub](https://esphome.io/components/output/bp1658cj#bp1658cj-component) as a float output.

components.name: Camera Encoder
components.description: The `camera_encoder` component provides image compression support for software-based cameras or cameras without internal compression. It allows raw camera frames to be compressed into a format suitable for transmission to API clients, such as Home Assistant, which expect JPEG-compressed images.

components.name: CAP1188 Capacitive Touch Sensor
components.description: The `cap1188` sensor platform allows you to use your CAP1188 ([datasheet](https://cdn-shop.adafruit.com/datasheets/CAP1188.pdf), [Adafruit](https://learn.adafruit.com/adafruit-cap1188-breakout/overview)) Capacitive Touch Sensor with ESPHome. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: CAP1188 Capacitive Touch Sensor
components.description: The `cap1188` binary sensor allows you to use your CAP1188 with ESPHome. First, setup a [Component/Hub](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor/cap1188#cap1188-component) and then use this binary sensor platform to create individual binary sensors for each touch sensor.

components.name: Captive Portal
components.description: The captive portal component in ESPHome is a fallback mechanism for when connecting to the configured [WiFi](https://esphome.io/components/wifi/) fails.

components.name: CC1101 Low-Power Sub-1 GHz RF Transceiver
components.description: The **CC1101** component provides a driver for the **Texas Instruments CC1101** Sub-1 GHz RF Transceiver ([datasheet](https://www.ti.com/lit/ds/symlink/cc1101.pdf)). The CC1101 is a low-cost, low-power radio chip commonly used for wireless communication in the 300-928 MHz frequency bands, including the popular 315 MHz, 433 MHz, 868 MHz, and 915 MHz ISM bands.

components.name: CCS811 eCO_2 and Volatile Organic Compound Sensor
components.description: The `ccs811` sensor platform allows you to use CCS811 eCO_2 and volatile organic compound sensors ([Adafruit](https://www.adafruit.com/product/3566)) with ESPHome.

components.name: cd74hc4067 Analog Multiplexer
components.description: The `cd74hc4067` Analog Multiplexer component allows you to attach up to 16 analog input sensors to a single ADC pin. The component is used to activate the right multiplexer pin for each of the defined cd74hc4067 sensors.

components.name: cd74hc4067 Analog Multiplexer
components.description: - **sensor** (**Required**, [ID](https://esphome.io/guides/configuration-types#id)): The source sensor to measure voltage values from, e.g. [ADC sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/adc/). - **cd74hc4067_id** (**Required**, [ID](https://esphome.io/guides/configuration-types#id)): The id of the cd74hc4067 component to use for this sensor. - **number** (**Required**, int): The number of the cd74hc4067 input pin (0-15) - All other options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor).

components.name: CH422G I/O Expander
components.description: The CH422G component allows you to use the **CH422G** I/O expander in ESPHome. It uses an [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) for communication. The I²C address is not configurable as the CH422G has a separate address for each internal register.

components.name: CH423 I/O Expander
components.description: The CH423 component allows you to use the **CH423** I/O expander in ESPHome. It uses an [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) for communication. The I²C address is not configurable as the CH423 has a separate address for each internal register.

components.name: chsc6x Touch Screen Controller
components.description: The `chsc6x` touchscreen platform allows using the touch screen controllers based on the chsc6x chip with ESPHome. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this touchscreen to work.

components.name: CUBIC CM1106 Single Beam NDIR CO2 Sensor Module
components.description: The `cm1106` sensor platform allows you to use CM1106 CO2 sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: Color Temperature Light
components.description: The `color_temperature` light platform creates a Color Temperature light from 2 [float output components](https://esphome.io/components/output/). One channel controls the LED temperature, and the other channel controls the brightness.

components.name: Combine the state of several sensors
components.description: The `combination` sensor platform allows you to combine the state of several sensors into one. To use this sensor, specify the combination type and add your source sensors.

components.name: CS5460A Power Sensor
components.description: The `cs5460a` sensor platform allows you to use a CS5460A AC voltage, current and power meter chip ([datasheet](https://statics.cirrus.com/pubs/proDatasheet/CS5460A_F5.pdf)) with ESPHome. The chip is usually sold on a breakout board with a 4.096 MHz crystal. However it requires a few extra components, specifically a current sensing circuit and a voltage sensing circuit.

components.name: CSE7761 Power Sensor
components.description: The `cse7761` sensor platform allows you to use your CSE7761 voltage/current and power sensors with ESPHome. This sensor is commonly found in Sonoff Dual R3 v1.x.

components.name: CSE7766 Power Sensor
components.description: The `cse7766` sensor platform allows you to use your CSE7766 voltage/current and power sensors ([datasheet](https://itead.cc/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/CSE7766.pdf)) sensors with ESPHome. This sensor is commonly found in Sonoff POW R2. CSE7759B is similar to CSE7766 and works with this component.

components.name: cst226 Touch Screen Controller
components.description: In addition to touch areas on the screen configured through the [Touchscreen](https://esphome.io/components/touchscreen#config-touchscreen) component, the cst226 supports a button located outside of the normal touchscreen area. A binary sensor can be configured to react to touching this button.

components.name: cst226 Touch Screen Controller
components.description: The `cst226` touchscreen platform allows using the touch screen controllers based on the cst226 chip with ESPHome. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this touchscreen to work.

components.name: cst816 Touch Screen Controller
components.description: The `cst816` touchscreen platform allows using the touch screen controllers based on the CST816 series of chips with ESPHome. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this touchscreen to work.

components.name: CT Clamp Current Sensor
components.description: The Current Transformer Clamp (`ct_clamp`  ) sensor allows you to hook up a CT Clamp to an analog voltage sensor (like the [ADC sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/adc/)) and convert the readings to measured single phase AC current.

components.name: Current Based Cover
components.description: The `current_based` cover platform allows you to create covers with position control by using current sensors to detect the fully-open and fully-closed states. This is pretty useful when using motors with integrated mechanical endstops. During cover operation, the component monitors the current consumption to detect when the motor has stopped.

components.name: Cold White + Warm White Light
components.description: The `cwww` light platform creates a cold white + warm white light from 2 [float output components](https://esphome.io/components/output/) (one for each channel). The two channels can be controlled individually or together.

components.name: DAC7678
components.description: The DAC7678 component represents a DAC7678 12-bit 8 channel DAC with internal reference ([datasheet](https://www.ti.com/lit/ds/symlink/dac7678.pdf)) in ESPHome. It uses the [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) for communication.

components.name: DAC7678
components.description: The DAC7678 output component exposes a DAC7678 DAC channel of a global [DAC7678 hub](https://esphome.io/components/output/dac7678#dac7678-component) as a float output.

components.name: Dallas Temperature Sensor
components.description: The `dallas_temp` component allows you to use [DS18B20](https://www.adafruit.com/product/374) ([datasheet](https://datasheets.maximintegrated.com/en/ds/DS18B20.pdf)) and similar 1-Wire temperature sensors. A [1-Wire bus](https://esphome.io/components/one_wire/) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Daly BMS Sensor
components.description: The `daly_bms` sensor platform allows you to use a Daly Smart BMS ([more info](https://dalyelec.en.made-in-china.com/)) with ESPHome.

components.name: Daly BMS Sensor
components.description: Binary sensor that indicates the status of MOS.

components.name: Debug Component
components.description: The `debug` component can be used to debug problems with ESPHome. At startup, it prints a bunch of useful information like reset reason, free heap size, ESPHome version and so on. It also allows you to get the same information as a text sensor, and to monitor the state of the ESP heap memory (free space, maximum free block size and fragmentation level) and the main-loop timing. Monitoring can be done via the logger or web server component if it has been configured.

components.description: The `debug` component can be used to debug problems with ESPHome. At startup, it prints a bunch of useful information like reset reason, free heap size, ESPHome version and so on. It also allows you to get the same information as a text sensor, and to monitor the state of the ESP heap memory (free space, maximum free block size and fragmentation level) and the main-loop timing. Monitoring can be done via the logger or web server component if it has been configured.

It also allows you to get the same information as a text sensor, and to monitor the state of the ESP heap memory (free space, maximum free block size and fragmentation level) and the main-loop timing. Monitoring can be done via the logger or web server component if it has been configured.

components.name: Deep Sleep Component
components.description: The `deep_sleep` component can be used to automatically enter a deep sleep mode on the ESP8266/ESP32/BK72xx/nRF52 after a certain amount of time. This is especially useful with nodes that operate on batteries and therefore need to conserve as much energy as possible.

components.name: Demo Component
components.description: The `demo` component can be used for testing to generate sample instances of many different components (sensors, lights, ...)

components.name: Dew Point
components.description: The `dew_point` platform allows you to calculate the dew point from air temperature and relative humidity. For the calculation the Magnus formula approximation is used.

components.name: DF-Player mini
components.description: The `dfplayer` ([datasheet](https://wiki.dfrobot.com/DFPlayer_Mini_SKU_DFR0299)), component allows you to play sound and music stored in an SD card or USB flash drive.

components.name: DFRobot mmWave Radar
components.description: The [DFRobot mmWave Radar](https://wiki.dfrobot.com/mmWave_Radar_Human_Presence_Detection_SKU_SEN0395) (aka `SEN0395`  ) is a 24GHz radar which can be used for human presence detection. It can detect tiny movements and compared to a PIR sensor **it can detect presence continuously**. This can be useful, for example, to turn the lights on when you enter a room, keep them on as long as you are there (without waving your hands at the sensor) and turn them off almost immediately after you leave the room.

components.name: DFRobot mmWave Radar
components.description: To determine presence, you can simply use a [Gpio](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor/gpio/) and connect a single GPIO pin from your processor to the IO2 pin of the mmWave radar. Using a dedicated GPIO pin may offer an advantage since it may wake the processor from sleep.

components.name: DFRobot mmWave Radar

components.name: DHT12 Temperature+Humidity Sensor

components.name: DLMS Meter
components.description: The `dlms_meter` component connects to smart meters which use the encrypted **DLMS/COSEM** protocol over UART. These smart meters are (for example) widely deployed in Austria.

components.name: DPS310/DPS368 Atmospheric Pressure Sensor
components.description: The `dps310` sensor platform allows you to use both the temperature and pressure sensors on your DPS310 or DPS368 atmospheric pressure sensor ([Adafruit](https://www.adafruit.com/product/4494)) with ESPHome. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) component is required to be set up in your configuration.

components.name: DS1307 Time Source

components.name: 1-Wire Bus via DS2484
components.description: The `ds2484` platform provides access to 1-Wire busses via DS2484 device using the [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) for communication.

components.name: DSMR Component
components.description: The DSMR component connects to Dutch Smart Meters which comply to DSMR (Dutch Smart Meter Requirements), also known as 'Slimme meter' or 'P1 port'.

components.name: Duty Cycle Sensor
components.description: The duty cycle sensor allows you to measure for what percentage of time a signal on a GPIO pin is HIGH or LOW.

components.name: Duty Time
components.description: The `duty_time` sensor allows you to track the total duty time of some object, for example, a light bulb, in seconds. Able to calculate the last turn-on time when an optional sensor `last_time` is included in the configuration.

components.name: EE895 CO₂, Temperature and Pressure Sensor
components.description: The `ee895` sensor platform allows you to use your EE895 CO₂ ([datasheet](https://www.epluse.com/fileadmin/data/product/ee895/BA_EE895.pdf), [E+E EE895](https://www.epluse.com/products/co2-measurement/co2-modules-and-probes/ee895/)) sensor with ESPHome. The [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work. We recommend the use of pull up resistors.

components.name: EKTF2232 Touchscreen Controller
components.description: The `ektf2232` component allows using the touchscreen controller found in the [Inkplate 6 Plus](https://esphome.io/components/display/inkplate/) with ESPHome. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: EMC2101 Fan Controller and Temperature sensor
components.description: The `emc2101` component allows you to control a cooling fan from ESPHome ([datasheet](https://ww1.microchip.com/downloads/aemDocuments/documents/MSLD/ProductDocuments/DataSheets/EMC2101-Data-Sheet-DS20006703.pdf)). It also provides temperature sensors and a tachometer. The fan can be controlled either by a PWM signal or by outputing a DC voltage using its DAC.

components.name: OpenEnergyMonitor EmonTx Sensors
components.description: The `emontx` component allows you to use ESPHome to create a connection to an OpenEnergyMonitor emonTX via a supported device (ESP32 recommended). This component is a global hub that establishes the connection to the EmonTx via [UART bus](https://esphome.io/components/uart) and translates the received data. Using the [emontx sensors](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/emontx#emontx-sensors), you can then create sensors for Home Assistant that track voltage, current, power as measured by CTs (up to 12), pulse data, and temperature depending on the configuration of the emonTX.

components.name: OpenEnergyMonitor EmonTx Sensors
components.description: The `emontx` component allows you to use ESPHome to create a connection to an OpenEnergyMonitor emonTX via a supported device (ESP32 recommended). This component is a global hub that establishes the connection to the EmonTx via [UART bus](https://esphome.io/components/uart) and translates the received data. Using the [emontx sensors](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/emontx#emontx-sensors), you can then create sensors for Home Assistant that track voltage, current, power as measured by CTs (up to 12), pulse data, and temperature depending on the configuration of the emonTX.

components.name: Endstop Cover
components.description: The `endstop` cover platform allows you to create covers with position control that have endstops at both ends of the cover to detect the fully-open and fully-closed states. When any of these endstops are reached, the cover is stopped (via `stop_action`  ) and the corresponding state is sent out.

components.name: ENS210 Temperature+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The `ENS210` Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your ENS210 ([datasheet](https://www.sciosense.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/ENS210-Datasheet.pdf)) i2c-based sensor with ESPHome.

components.name: ES7210
components.description: The `es7210` platform allows your ESPHome devices to use the ES7210 high performance four channel audio ADC ([datasheet](http://www.everest-semi.com/pdf/ES7210%20PB.pdf)). This allows attached microphones to be used as a microphone input via [I2S Audio](https://esphome.io/components/microphone/i2s_audio/).

components.name: ES7243E
components.description: The `es7243e` platform allows your ESPHome devices to use the ES7243E high performance four channel audio ADC ([datasheet](https://www.lcsc.com/datasheet/lcsc_datasheet_2410121611_Everest-semi-Everest-Semiconductor-ES7243E_C2929446.pdf)). This allows attached microphones to be used as a microphone input via [I2S Audio](https://esphome.io/components/microphone/i2s_audio/).

components.name: ES8156
components.description: The `es8156` platform allows your ESPHome devices to use the ES8156 low power mono audio codec. This allows the playback of audio via the microcontroller from a range of sources via [Speaker](https://esphome.io/components/speaker/) or [Media Player](https://esphome.io/components/media_player/).

components.name: ES8311
components.description: The `es8311` platform allows your ESPHome devices to use the ES8311 low power mono audio codec. This allows the playback of audio via the microcontroller from a range of sources via [Speaker](https://esphome.io/components/speaker/) or [Media Player](https://esphome.io/components/media_player/).

components.name: ES8388
components.description: None

components.name: ES8388
components.description: The `es8388` select allows you to control the `dac output` and the `adc input_mic` of your [Es8388](https://esphome.io/components/audio_dac/es8388/).

components.name: ESP32 Platform
components.description: This component contains platform-specific options for the ESP32 platform.

components.name: BLE Component
components.description: The `esp32_ble` component in ESPHome sets up the Bluetooth LE stack on the device so that a [Esp32 Ble Server](https://esphome.io/components/esp32_ble_server/) can run.

components.name: ESP32 Bluetooth Low Energy Beacon
components.description: The `esp32_ble_beacon` component creates a Bluetooth Low Energy Beacon with your ESP32 device. Beacons are BLE devices that repeatedly just send out a pre-defined packet of data. This packet can then be received by devices like smartphones and can then be used to track a phone's location.

components.name: BLE Server
components.description: The `esp32_ble_server` component in ESPHome sets up a BLE GATT server that exposes the device name, manufacturer and board. BLE GATT services and characteristics can be added to the server to expose data and control.

components.name: ESP32 Bluetooth Low Energy Tracker Hub
components.description: The `esp32_ble_tracker` component creates a global hub so that you can track bluetooth low energy devices using your ESP32 node.

components.name: ESP32 Camera Component
components.description: The `esp32_camera` component allows you to use ESP32-based camera boards in ESPHome that directly integrate into Home Assistant through the native API.

components.name: ESP32 Camera Web Server Component
components.description: The `esp32_camera_web_server` component allows you to use expose web server of ESP32-based camera boards in ESPHome that directly can be integrated into external surveillance or PVR software.

components.name: ESP32 CAN
components.description: The ESP32 has an integrated CAN controller and therefore doesn't necessarily need an external controller. Some variants (ESP32-C5, ESP32-C6, ESP32-P4) have multiple CAN controllers - see [Multiple CAN Controllers](https://esphome.io/components/canbus/esp32_can#multiple-can-controllers) below. You only need to specify the RX and TX pins. Any GPIO will work.

components.name: ESP32 DAC
components.description: The ESP32 DAC platform allows you to output analog voltages using the 8-bit digital-to-analog converter of the ESP32. Unlike the [Ledc](https://esphome.io/components/output/ledc/), which can simulate an analog signal by using a fast switching frequency, the hardware DAC can output a *real* analog signal with no need for additional filtering.

components.name: ESP32 Hosted
components.description: ESP32 Hosted ([ESP-Hosted-MCU](https://github.com/espressif/esp-hosted-mcu)) is a solution that allows you to use ESP32 modules as communication co-processors. This solution provides wireless connectivity (Wi-Fi and Bluetooth) to the host module, enabling it to communicate with other devices.

components.name: ESP32 Hosted Co-processor Update
components.description: ESP32 Hosted update platform to manage co-processor firmware updates.

components.name: Improv via BLE
components.description: The `esp32_improv` component in ESPHome implements the open [Improv standard](https://www.improv-wifi.com/) for configuring Wi-Fi on an ESP32 device by using Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) to receive the credentials.

components.name: ESP32 RMT LED Strip
components.description: This is a component using the ESP32 RMT peripheral to drive most addressable LED strips.

components.name: ESP32 Touch Pad

components.name: ESP32 Touch Pad

components.name: ESP8266 Platform
components.description: This component contains platform-specific options for the ESP8266 platform.

components.name: ESP8266 Software PWM Output
components.description: The ESP8266 Software PWM platform allows you to use a software PWM on the pins GPIO0-GPIO16 on your ESP8266. Note that this is a software PWM, so there can be some flickering during periods of high WiFi activity. Hardware PWMs like the one on the ESP32 (see [Ledc](https://esphome.io/components/output/ledc/)) are preferred.

components.name: ESP32-P4 LDO
components.description: The ESP32-P4 has 4 Low-Dropout linear regulators that can be used to power on- and off-chip peripherals. This component allows those regulators to be configured and enabled.

components.name: ESPHome Core Configuration
components.description: Here you specify some core information that ESPHome needs to create firmwares. Most importantly, this is the section of the configuration where you specify the **name** of the node.

components.name: ESPHome OTA Updates
components.description: ESPHome's Over-The-Air (OTA) platform to allow remote updating of devices.

components.name: ESPNow communication Component
components.description: The ESPNow component allows ESPHome to communicate with esp32 devices in a simple and unrestricted way. It enables the option to interact with other esp32 devices over the Espressif's ESP-NOW protocol, see [documentation](https://docs.espressif.com/projects/esp-idf/en/stable/esp32/api-reference/network/esp_now.html). It can be used with the [Packet Transport Component](https://esphome.io/components/packet_transport) to broadcast sensor data, see [ESP-NOW Packet Transport Platform](https://esphome.io/components/packet_transport/espnow).

components.name: ESP-NOW Packet Transport Platform

components.name: Ethernet Component
components.description: This ESPHome component enables *wired* Ethernet connections for ESP32 and RP2040 boards.

components.name: Ethernet Info Text Sensor
components.description: The `ethernet_info` text sensor platform exposes different Ethernet information via text sensors.

components.name: Exposure Notification Listener
components.description: The `exposure_notifications` component uses the [Esp32 Ble Tracker](https://esphome.io/components/esp32_ble_tracker/) to discover nearby COVID-19 exposure notification bluetooth messages sent by phones running the [Google/Apple Exposure Notification service](https://www.google.com/covid19/exposurenotifications/).

components.name: External Components
components.description: You can easily import community or personal components using the external components feature. Bundled components can be overridden using this feature.

components.name: EZO sensor circuits
components.description: The `ezo` sensor platform allows you to use your EZO sensor circuits with ESPHome. The [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work. All embedded solutions from EZO can be found [on their website](https://atlas-scientific.com/embedded-solutions/). If a certain command is not supported directly, it can be executed with the `send_custom()` method call.

components.name: Atlas Scientific Peristaltic Pump
components.description: The `ezo_pmp` component allows you to use an Atlas Scientific Peristaltic Pump with ESPHome. Both the EZO-PMP ([datasheet](https://files.atlas-scientific.com/EZO_PMP_Datasheet.pdf)) and EZO-PMP-L ([datasheet](https://files.atlas-scientific.com/EZO_PMP_L_Datasheet.pdf)) are supported. The [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: FastLED Light
components.description: The `fastled_clockless` light platform allows you to create RGB lights in ESPHome for a [number of supported chipsets](https://esphome.io/components/light/fastled#fastled_clockless-chipsets).

components.name: Feedback Cover
components.description: The `feedback` cover platform allows you to create covers with position control that can optionally have feedback from sensors to detect the fully-open and fully-closed states (endstops), and from sensors to detect actual movement (opening/closing). In all the cases the current position is approximated with the time the cover has been moving in a direction.

components.name: Grow Fingerprint Reader

components.name: Font Renderer Component

You have the option to use **any** OpenType/TrueType (`.ttf`, `.otf`, `.woff`  ) font file at **any** size, as well as fixed-size [PCF](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portable_Compiled_Format) and [BDF](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glyph_Bitmap_Distribution_Format) bitmap fonts.

components.name: FT5X06 Touch Screen Controller

components.name: I²C Bus
components.description: This component sets up the I²C bus for your ESP32, ESP8266, RP2040 or NRF52. In order for these components to work correctly, you need to define the I²C bus in your configuration. On ESP32 (both Arduino and ESP-IDF frameworks), internal pullup resistors are enabled by default. On ESP8266, the internal 10kΩ pullup resistors are always enabled. External pullups are still recommended for longer wire runs or multiple devices. You can use multiple devices on one I²C bus as each device is given a unique address for communicating between it and the ESP. You can do this by hopping wires from the two lines (SDA and SCL) from each device board to the next device board or by connecting the wires from each device back to the two I²C pins on the ESP.

components.name: MCP3008 8-Channel 10-Bit A/D Converter

components.name: MCP3008 8-Channel 10-Bit A/D Converter

First, setup a [MCP3008 Hub](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/mcp3008#mcp3008-component) for your MCP3008 sensor and then use this sensor platform to create individual sensors that will report the voltage to Home Assistant.

components.name: MCP3204 & MCP3208 12-Bit A/D Converters

This component is common for both device types. Configure both devices as MCP3204 and observe the maximum number of channels as per variant used.

components.name: MCP3204 & MCP3208 12-Bit A/D Converters

components.name: MCP4461 Component
components.description: The MCP4461 output component enables the use of an 8‑bit external quad digital potentiometer/rheostat via I²C. See the [MCP4461 Datasheet](https://www.microchip.com/en-us/product/mcp4461) for more information.

components.name: MCP4461 Component
components.description: The MCP4461 output component exposes 8 MCP4461 (wiper-)channels of a global [MCP4461](https://esphome.io/components/output/mcp4461#mcp4461-component) as float outputs.

components.name: MCP4725 Output
components.description: The MCP4725 output component allows to use [12bit external DAC](https://learn.sparkfun.com/tutorials/mcp4725-digital-to-analog-converter-hookup-guide/all) in order to have analog output(s) on any board by using I2C. Devices default address is `0x60` and configurable alternative is `0x61`.

components.name: MCP4728 Component
components.description: The MCP4728 output component allows to use [12bit external quad DAC](https://www.adafruit.com/product/4470) in order to have analog outputs on any board by using [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c). Devices default address is `0x60`.

components.name: MCP4728 Component
components.description: The MCP4728 output component exposes 4 MCP4728 channels of a global [MCP4728](https://esphome.io/components/output/mcp4728#mcp4728-component) as float outputs.

components.name: MCP47A1 Output
components.description: The `mcp47a1` output component allows to use [6bit external DAC](https://www.microchip.com/en-us/product/MCP47A1) in order to have analog output(s) on any board by using I²C. Devices default address is `0x2E` and configurable alternative is `0x3E`.

components.name: MCP9600 Thermocouple Amplifier
components.description: The `mcp9600` sensor platform allows you to use your MCP9600 and MCP9601 ([datasheet](https://cdn-learn.adafruit.com/assets/assets/000/105/040/original/MCP960X-Data-Sheet-20005426%281%29.pdf), [Adafruit](https://www.adafruit.com/product/4101)) to measure the temperature of a connected thermocouple while also measuring the ambient temperature around the sensor with ESPHome. [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: MCP9808 Temperature Sensor

components.name: Micro Wake Word

components.name: Modbus Component
components.description: The Modbus protocol is used by many consumer and industrial devices for communication. This component allows components in ESPHome to communicate to those devices via RTU protocol. You can access the coils, inputs, holding, read registers from your devices as sensors, switches, selects, numbers or various other ESPHome components and present them to your favorite Home Automation system. You can even write them as binary or float ouptputs from ESPHome.

components.name: Modbus Controller
components.description: The `modbus_controller` component creates a RS485 connection to control a Modbus server (slave) device, letting your ESPHome node act as a Modbus client (master). You can access the coils, inputs, holding, read registers from your devices as sensors, switches, selects, numbers or various other ESPHome components and present them to your favorite Home Automation system. You can even write them as binary or float outputs from ESPHome.

components.description: The `monochromatic` light platform creates a simple brightness-only light from a [float output component](https://esphome.io/components/output/).

components.description: The `mopeka_pro_check` sensor platform lets you track the output of Mopeka Pro Check LP, Mopeka Pro Plus, Mopeka Pro Universal or Lippert Propane Tank Sensors, Bluetooth Low Energy devices using the [Esp32 Ble Tracker](https://esphome.io/components/esp32_ble_tracker/). This component will track the tank level, distance, temperature, battery percentage, and sensor reading quality of a device every time the sensor sends out a BLE broadcast. There are additional configuration options to control handling of poor quality readings and reporting reading quality issues.

components.name: Mopeka Standard Check BLE Sensor
components.description: The `mopeka_std_check` sensor platform lets you track the output of Mopeka Standard Check LP Bluetooth Low Energy devices using the [Esp32 Ble Tracker](https://esphome.io/components/esp32_ble_tracker/). This component will track the tank level, distance, temperature, and battery percentage of a Mopeka Standard Check LP BLE device every time the sensor sends out a BLE broadcast.

components.name: MPL3115A2 Barometric Pressure/Altitude/Temperature Sensor
components.description: The `mpl3115a2` sensor platform allows you to use your MPL3115A2 atmospheric pressure sensors ([datasheet](https://www.nxp.com/docs/en/data-sheet/MPL3115A2.pdf), [Adafruit](https://www.adafruit.com/product/1893)) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: MPR121 Capacitive Touch Sensor
components.description: The `mpr121` sensor platform allows you to use your MPR121 ([datasheet](https://cdn-learn.adafruit.com/downloads/pdf/adafruit-mpr121-12-key-capacitive-touch-sensor-breakout-tutorial.pdf), [Adafruit](https://learn.adafruit.com/adafruit-mpr121-12-key-capacitive-touch-sensor-breakout-tutorial/overview)) Capacitive Touch Sensor with ESPHome. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: MPR121 Capacitive Touch Sensor
components.description: The `mpr121` binary sensor allows you to use your MPR121 with ESPHome. First, setup a [Component/Hub](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor/mpr121#mpr121-component) and then use this binary sensor platform to create individual binary sensors for each touch sensor.

components.name: MPU6050 Accelerometer/Gyroscope Sensor
components.description: The `mpu6050` sensor platform allows you to use your MPU6050 Accelerometer/Gyroscope ([datasheet](https://www.invensense.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/02/MPU-6000-Datasheet1.pdf), [SparkFun](https://www.sparkfun.com/products/11028)) sensors with ESPHome. The [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: MPU6886 Accelerometer/Gyroscope Sensor
components.description: The `mpu6886` sensor platform allows you to use your MPU6886 Accelerometer/Gyroscope ([datasheet](https://m5stack.oss-cn-shenzhen.aliyuncs.com/resource/docs/datasheet/core/MPU-6886-000193%2Bv1.1_GHIC_en.pdf), [M5Stack](https://docs.m5stack.com/en/unit/imu)) sensors with ESPHome. The [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: MQTT Client Component
components.description: The MQTT Client Component sets up the MQTT connection to your broker. If you are connecting to Home Assistant, you may prefer to use the native API, in which case this is not needed.

components.name: MQTT Subscribe Sensor
components.description: The `mqtt_subscribe` sensor platform allows you to get external data into ESPHome. The sensor will subscribe to messages on the given MQTT topic and parse each message into a floating point number.

components.name: MQTT Subscribe Text Sensor
components.description: The `mqtt_subscribe` text sensor platform allows you to get external data into ESPHome. The sensor will subscribe to messages on the given MQTT topic and save the most recent value in its `id(mysensor).state`.

components.name: MS5611 Atmospheric Pressure Sensor
components.description: The `ms5611` sensor platform allows you to use the MS5611 atmospheric pressure-and-temperature sensor with ESPHome (see the [datasheet](https://www.te.com/commerce/DocumentDelivery/DDEController?Action=showdoc&DocId=Data+Sheet%7FMS5611-01BA03%7FB3%7Fpdf%7FEnglish%7FENG_DS_MS5611-01BA03_B3.pdf%7FCAT-BLPS0036) for technical details). The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: MS8607 Temperature+Pressure+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The `ms8607` sensor platform allows you to use your MS8607 ([datasheet](https://www.te.com/commerce/DocumentDelivery/DDEController?Action=srchrtrv&DocNm=MS8607-02BA01&DocType=DS&DocLang=English), [Adafruit](https://www.adafruit.com/product/4716)) temperature, pressure and humidity sensors with ESPHome. An [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: MSA301 and MSA311 Sensors
components.description: The `msa3xx` sensor platform allows you to use your MSA301 and MSA311 tri-axial, low-g accelerometers ([datasheet](https://cdn-shop.adafruit.com/product-files/5309/MSA311-V1.1-ENG.pdf)) with ESPHome. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: MSA301 and MSA311 Sensors
components.description: Three binary sensors available for use. Internal 500 ms debounce is applied for all sensors. For every sensor **name** is required. All other options from [Binary Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor#config-binary_sensor). Shorthand notation also can be used.

components.name: MSA301 and MSA311 Sensors
components.description: Acceleration data is available through sensors configuration. You can use shorthand notation like `acceleration_x: "Acceleration X"` or use regular notation. For regular notation only the **name** is required. All options from [Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/sensor).

components.name: MSA301 and MSA311 Sensors
components.description: Text sensor provides orientation information. You can use shorthand notation like `orientation_xy: "Orientation XY"` or use regular notation.

components.name: MY9231/MY9291 LED driver
components.description: The MY9231/MY9291 component represents a MY9231/MY9291 LED driver chain ([MY9231 description](http://www.my-semi.com.tw/file/MY9231_BF_0.91.pdf), [MY9291 description](http://www.my-semi.com.tw/file/MY9291_BF_0.91.pdf)) in ESPHome. Communication is done with two GPIO pins (DI and DCKI) and multiple driver chips can be chained. There are two models with different number of output channels (MY9291 with 4 channels and MY9231 with 3 channels). They are popular driver chips used in smart light bulbs:

components.name: MY9231/MY9291 LED driver
components.description: The MY931/MY9291 output component exposes a MY931/MY9291 channel of a global [Component/Hub](https://esphome.io/components/output/my9231#my9231-component) as a float output.

components.name: NAU7802 24-bit ADC
components.description: The `nau7802` sensor platform allows you to use your NAU7802 ADC ([datasheet](https://www.nuvoton.com/resource-files/NAU7802%20Data%20Sheet%20V1.7.pdf), [Adafruit](https://www.adafruit.com/product/4538)) with ESPHome. This ADC is typically used with strain gauges to act as a weight sensor.

components.name: NeoPixelBus Light
components.description: > [!WARNING] > NeoPixelBus does **not** work with ESP-IDF. > > For clockless lights, you can use [Esp32 Rmt Led Strip](https://esphome.io/components/light/esp32_rmt_led_strip/), and for SPI LEDs see [Spi Led Strip](https://esphome.io/components/light/spi_led_strip/).

components.name: Network component
components.description: The network component is a global configuration for all types of networks (WiFi, Ethernet).

components.name: Nextion Binary Sensor Component
components.description: The `nextion`   binary sensor platform supports the many switched components in the Nextion as well as integer variables (>0 == true). It can be a component or variable in the Nextion display. It is best to set the components vscope to global in the Nextion Editor. This way the component will be available if the page is shown or not.

components.name: Nextion TFT LCD Display
components.description: The `nextion` display platform allows you to use Nextion LCD displays ([datasheet](https://nextion.itead.cc/resources/datasheets/), [iTead](https://www.itead.cc/display/nextion.html)) with ESPHome.

components.name: Nextion Sensor Component
components.description: The `nextion` sensor platform supports integers or floats (Xfloat). It can be a component, a variable or a waveform in the Nextion display. It is best to set the component's vscope to global in the Nextion Editor. This way the component will be available if the page is shown or not.

components.name: Nextion Switch Component
components.description: The `nextion` switch platform supports the many switched components in the Nextion as well as integer variables. It can be a component or variable in the Nextion display. It is best to set the components vscope to global in the Nextion Editor. This way the component will be available if the page is shown or not.

components.name: Nextion Text Sensor Component
components.description: The `nextion` text sensor platform supports text strings. It can be a component or variable in the Nextion display. It is best to set the components vscope to global in the Nextion Editor. This way the component will be available if the page is shown or not.

components.name: NFC Binary Sensor
components.description: The `nfc` binary sensor platform provides an easy way for you to determine if an NFC tag is presented to the reader. The tag may be identified in one of three ways:

components.name: NPI-19 Pressure Sensor
components.description: The `npi19` sensor platform allows you to use your NPI-19 ([datasheet](https://www.amphenol-sensors.com/hubfs/AAS-920-699F-NovaSensor-NPI-19-I2C-061322-web.pdf), [product page](https://www.amphenol-sensors.com/en/novasensor/pressure-sensors/3358-npi-19-i2c)) pressure sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: NRF52 Platform
components.description: This component contains platform-specific options for the NRF52 platform.

components.name: NTC Sensor
components.description: The `ntc` platform is a helper sensor that allows you to convert resistance readings from a NTC thermistor to temperature readings.

components.name: Number Sensor
components.description: The Number Sensor platform allows you to view the state of any number component as a read-only sensor.

components.name: Online Image Component
components.description: With this component you can define images that will be downloaded, decoded and drawn at runtime.

components.name: OpenTherm
components.description: OpenTherm (OT) is a standard communications protocol used in central heating systems for the communication between central heating appliances and a thermostatic controller. As a standard, OpenTherm is independent of any single manufacturer. A controller from manufacturer A can in principle be used to control a boiler from manufacturer B.

components.name: OpenTherm
components.description: The component can report boiler status on several binary sensors. The *Status* sensors are updated in each message cycle, while the others are only set during initialization, as they are unlikely to change without restarting the boiler.

components.name: OpenTherm
components.description: OpenTherm bridge in ESPHome.

components.name: OpenThread Component
components.description: [Thread](https://www.threadgroup.org) is a low-power mesh networking standard for IoT devices. The low-power aspect is important for battery-powered smart home devices. However, it’s also low-bandwidth, making it ideal for applications that don’t send a lot of data, like switches or motion sensors.

components.name: OpenThread Info Text Sensor
components.description: The `openthread_info` text sensor platform exposes different OpenThread network information via text sensors.

components.name: OPT3001 Ambient Light Sensor
components.description: The `opt3001` sensor platform allows you to use your OPT3001 ([datasheet](https://www.ti.com/lit/ds/symlink/opt3001.pdf)) ambient light sensor with ESPHome. The [I²C bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Generic Output Button
components.description: The `output` button platform allows you to use any output component as a button. This can for example be used to momentarily set a GPIO pin using a button.

components.name: Generic Output Lock
components.description: The `output` lock platform allows you to use any output component as a lock.

components.name: Generic Output Switch
components.description: The `output` switch platform allows you to use any output component as a switch.

components.name: Packages
components.description: When you have many ESPHome devices (or are producing and distributing them at scale), a common need tends to surface: configuration modularization. You'll likely want to break your configuration into common (groups of) elements, building it into reusable pieces which can subsequently be used by many/all devices. Only unique pieces of your configuration remain in any given device's YAML configuration file.

components.name: Packet Transport Binary Sensor
components.description: The `packet_transport` binary sensor platform allows you to receive binary sensor data directly from another ESPHome node. It requires a `packet_transport` component to be configured.

components.name: Packet Transport Sensor
components.description: The `packet_transport` sensor platform allows you to receive numeric sensor data directly from another ESPHome node. It requires a `packet_transport` component to be configured.

components.name: Light Partition
components.description: The `partition` light platform allows you to combine multiple addressable light segments (like [Fastled](https://esphome.io/components/light/fastled/) or [Neopixelbus](https://esphome.io/components/light/neopixelbus/)) and/or individual lights (like [Rgb](https://esphome.io/components/light/rgb/)) into a single addressable light. This platform also allows splitting up an addressable light into multiple segments, so that segments can be individually controlled.

components.name: PCA6416A I/O Expander
components.description: The PCA6416A component allows you to use **PCA6416A** or **PCAL6416A** I/O expanders in ESPHome. It uses [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) for communication.

components.name: PCA9554 I/O Expander
components.description: The PCA9554 component allows you to use a variety of Texas Instrument I/O expanders in ESPHome using the [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) for communication.

components.name: PCA9685
components.description: The PCA9685 component represents a PCA9685 12-bit PWM driver ([datasheet](https://www.nxp.com/docs/en/data-sheet/PCA9685.pdf), [adafruit](https://www.adafruit.com/product/815)) in ESPHome. It uses [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) for communication.

components.name: PCA9685
components.description: The PCA9685 output component exposes a PCA9685 PWM channel of a global [PCA9685 hub](https://esphome.io/components/output/pca9685#pca9685-component) as a float output.

components.name: PCD 8544 Display (Nokia 5110/3310)
components.description: The `pcd8544` display platform allows you to use PCD8544 ([Adafruit](https://www.adafruit.com/product/338)) displays with ESPHome. Connection to this display is made using the 4-Wire [SPI bus](https://esphome.io/components/spi).

components.name: PCF85063 Time Source
components.description: You first need to set up the [I2C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c/) component.

components.name: PCF8563 Time Source
components.description: You first need to set up the [I2C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c/) component.

components.name: PCF8574 I/O Expander
components.description: The PCF8574 component allows you to use PCF8574 or PCF8575 I/O expanders ([datasheet](http://www.ti.com/lit/ds/symlink/pcf8574.pdf), [SparkFun](https://www.sparkfun.com/products/retired/8130)) in ESPHome. It uses [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) for communication.

components.name: PI4IOE5V6408 8-Bit I2C I/O Expander
components.description: The PI4IOE5V6408 component allows you to use the Diodes Incorporated PI4IOE5V6408 8-bit I2C I/O expander in ESPHome using the [I²C Bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) for communication.

components.name: PID Climate
components.description: The `pid` climate platform allows you to regulate a value with a [PID controller](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PID_controller).

components.name: PID Climate
components.description: Additionally, the PID climate platform provides an optional sensor platform to monitor the calculated PID parameters to help finding good PID values.

components.name: PipSolar PV Inverter
components.description: The PipSolar component allows you to integrate PIP-compatible Inverters in ESPHome. It uses [UART](https://esphome.io/components/uart) for communication.

components.name: PM1006 Particulate Matter Sensor
components.description: The `pm1006` sensor platform allows you to use Cubic PM1006 particulate matter sensors ([datasheet](http://www.jdscompany.co.kr/download.asp?gubun=07&filename=PM1006_LED_PARTICLE_SENSOR_MODULE_SPECIFICATIONS.pdf)) with ESPHome.

components.name: CUBIC PM2005/PM2105 Laser Particle Sensor Module
components.description: The `pm2005` sensor platform allows you to use your PM2005/PM2105 laser particulate matter sensor with ESPHome.

components.name: PMSA003I Particulate Matter Sensor
components.description: The `pmsa003i` sensor platform allows you to use your Plantower PMSA003I particulate matter sensor ([datasheet](https://cdn-shop.adafruit.com/product-files/4632/4505_PMSA003I_series_data_manual_English_V2.6.pdf), [Adafruit](https://www.adafruit.com/product/4632)) with ESPHome.

components.name: PMSX003 Particulate Matter Sensor
components.description: The `pmsx003` sensor platform allows you to use your [Plantower](https://www.plantower.com/en/products_33/) [PMS1003](https://www.plantower.com/static/upload/file/20220627/1656292073878896.pdf), [PMS3003](https://www.dynamoelectronics.com/descargas/PMS3003.pdf), [PMS5003](https://www.aqmd.gov/docs/default-source/aq-spec/resources-page/plantower-pms5003-manual_v2-3.pdf), PMS5003S, PMS5003T, [PMS5003ST](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Arduinolibrary/DFRobot_SEN0233_Air_Quality_Monitor/master/PMS5003ST%20series%20data%20manua_English_V2.6%20.pdf), [PMS6003](https://www.laskakit.cz/user/related_files/203-pms6003.pdf), [PMS7003](https://download.kamami.pl/p564008-PMS7003%20series%20data%20manua_English_V2.5.pdf), [PMS9003M](https://evelta.com/content/datasheets/203-PMS9003M.pdf), [PMSA003](https://evelta.com/content/datasheets/PMSA003%20series%20data%20manual_English_V2.5.pdf), laser based particulate matter sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: PMWCS3 Capacitive Soil Moisture and Temperature Sensor
components.description: The `pmwcs3` sensor platform allows you to use your PMWCS3 ([informations](https://tinovi.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/PM-WCS-3-I2C.pdf)) capacitive soil moisture and temperature sensor with ESPHome. The [I²C bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work. Wiring scheme: red is 3.3V, black & shield is GND, green is SDA and white is SCL. 2.2kOhm resistors are advised to pullup both SDA & SDA lines.

components.name: PN532 NFC/RFID
components.description: The `pn532` component allows you to use PN532 NFC/RFID controllers ([datasheet](https://cdn-shop.adafruit.com/datasheets/pn532ds.pdf), [Adafruit](https://www.adafruit.com/product/364)) with ESPHome. This component is a global hub that establishes the connection to the PN532 via [SPI](https://esphome.io/components/spi) or [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) and outputs its data. Using the [PN532 binary sensors](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor/pn532#pn532-tag) you can then create individual binary sensors that track if an NFC/RFID tag is currently detected by the PN532.

components.name: PN7160 NFC
components.description: The `pn7160_spi` component allows you to use [SPI-equipped](https://esphome.io/components/spi) PN7160 NFC controllers with ESPHome.

components.name: Power Supply Component
components.description: The `power_supply` component allows you to have a high power mode for certain outputs. For example, if you're using an [ATX powersupply](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ATX) to power your LED strips, you usually don't want to have the power supply on all the time while the output is not on. The power supply component can be attached to any [Output Component](https://esphome.io/components/output/) and will automatically switch on if any of the outputs are on. Furthermore, it also has a cooldown time that keeps the power supply on for a while after the last output has been disabled.

components.name: ESPHome Core Configuration
components.description: This component is used to store data in the flash memory which is persisted across device reboots, e.g. the latest state of a light or the accumulated energy used by an appliance.

components.name: Prometheus Component
components.description: The `prometheus` component enables an HTTP endpoint for the [Web Server](https://esphome.io/components/web_server/) in order to integrate a [Prometheus](https://prometheus.io/) installation.

components.name: PSRAM
components.description: This component enables and configures PSRAM if/when available on ESP32 modules/boards. Some components require PSRAM, while others may use it for optional features. In any case it is required to explicitly configured - this is a change from previous versions of ESPHome.

components.name: Pulse Counter Sensor
components.description: The pulse counter sensor allows you to count the number of pulses and the frequency of a signal on any pin.

components.name: Pulse Meter Sensor
components.description: The pulse meter sensor allows you to count the number and frequency of pulses on any pin. It is intended to be a drop-in replacement for the [pulse counter component](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/pulse_counter/). Rather than counting pulses over a fixed time interval, the pulse meter sensor measures the time between pulses. The precise manner in which this is done depends on the `internal_filter_mode` option. This leads to a higher resolution, especially for low pulse rates, as the pulse counter sensor is limited by the number of pulses within a time interval.

components.name: Pulse Width Sensor
components.description: The `pulse_width` sensor allows you to measure how long a given digital signal is HIGH. For example this can be used to measure PWM signals to transmit some value over a simple protocol. The unit of measurement for this sensor is seconds.

components.name: PVVX MiThermometer Display
components.description: The `pvvx_mithermometer` display platform allows you to use devices running the [ATC_MiThermometer firmware](https://github.com/pvvx/ATC_MiThermometer) by pvvx as display drivers with ESPHome.

components.name: PVVX MiThermometer Display

This sensor is very similar to the [HMC5883L](https://esphome.io/components/sensor/hmc5883l#hmc5883l) sensor and is often found as a knock off replacement. The QMC5883L sensor performs on par to the HMC5883L sensor, though the configuration differs. The [I²C](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.
